body: {"decision":"warn","decision_source":"pre_claim_intake","detail":"child bead states a distinct 'part 3 of 3' scope but never specifies what differentiates it from parts 1 and 2: This is bead 3 of a 3-way decomposition of ddx-24949caf, but the description never states which functional slice of the parent fix belongs to this child. The ROOT CAUSE/PROPOSED FIX block reproduces the parent's entire fix verbatim (bounded wait on supervisorDone in server.go:379-385, the already-bounded worker cleanup in workers.go:1696-1703, and the missing systemd ExecStop/TimeoutStopSec mitigation in server_service.go:26-42) without narrowing to a specific part. The trailing NON-SCOPE line explicitly says 'Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads,' which presupposes a per-child split that is never actually written down anywhere in this bead's text. An agent given only this bead cannot tell whether it owns the Go supervisor-wait change, the systemd unit change, both, or something else — so it cannot safely pick a bounded diff without risking duplicate or conflicting work against parts 1 and 2. The description also contradicts the bead's own metadata: prose states 'Parent: none. Dependencies: none.' while the structured fields show parent=ddx-24949caf and depth=1, which is leftover text copied from the parent bead and not corrected for this child.","fingerprint":"4880756fbe16af46e778dc39729320db05de6f2a86da604867ca30ee4ca0e660","policy_mode":"warn-only","reason":"readiness_best_effort","rule_id":"pre_claim_intake.readiness_best_effort","suggested_action":"revise the rewrite so it preserves every explicit commitment","warning_fingerprint":"4880756fbe16af46e778dc39729320db05de6f2a86da604867ca30ee4ca0e660"}

{"decision":"warn","decision_source":"pre_claim_intake","detail":"description does not state what specifically distinguishes 'part 3 of 3' from sibling child beads' scope.: The description states this child 'owns part 3 of 3' of parent ddx-24949caf, but nowhere specifies what distinguishes part 3 from parts 1 and 2 — the ROOT CAUSE and PROPOSED FIX sections describe the entire unified fix (bound the supervisor wait, fall through to supervisorRegistry.Shutdown()/workers.Shutdown() on timeout, log the timeout) with no partition line. The NON-SCOPE clause says 'Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads' without naming any of them, so an executing agent cannot tell which piece of the fix is actually in scope here versus already done (or pending) in sibling beads, risking duplicate or conflicting implementation. Acceptance criteria are also generic whole-package test/lint gates with no named Test* function or -run filter validating the new bounded-wait behavior specifically. Root cause file:line evidence, parent/dependency fields, and non-scope constraints are otherwise present and well-formed. Four prior attempts all failed with 'session closed before Stop hook' / outcome_reason=unknown, which reads as session/infra flakiness rather than a bead-content issue and is not weighted into this classification.","fingerprint":"64a5a2058ae7f1a803ea916b3e0b4acdfbeb059e33d022f2dd1439e98996659a","policy_mode":"warn-only","reason":"readiness_best_effort","rule_id":"pre_claim_intake.readiness_best_effort","suggested_action":"revise the rewrite so it preserves every explicit commitment","warning_fingerprint":"64a5a2058ae7f1a803ea916b3e0b4acdfbeb059e33d022f2dd1439e98996659a"}
